updates.author: Yann LeCun (杨立昆)
updates.text: Super-fun in-depth discussion 🙏🙏🙏 World models, JEPA vs generative architectures, anti-collapse methods for JEPA SSL including contrastive, distillation, and information maximization methods, an explanation of SIGReg/LeJEPA/LeWM. Plus some bits on AMI Labs, Project Tapestry, as well as Lisp and homoiconic languages 😅

updates.author: OpenAI
updates.text: GPT-Live is now fully rolled out to all ChatGPT users on Go, Plus, and Pro plans. Free user rollout is in progress. Update to the latest version of the ChatGPT app on iOS or Android to try it out.

updates.author: OpenAI
updates.text: As coding models improve, evals need to become harder, fairer, and more trustworthy. Better benchmarks help the field understand real progress and where the frontier is moving.

updates.author: OpenAI
updates.text: To audit SWE-Bench Pro, we used model-based investigator agents alongside independent reviews from five independent experienced software engineers. That helped us examine tasks at scale while keeping expert judgment at the center.

updates.author: OpenAI
updates.text: Our audit of SWE-Bench Pro found that a meaningful share of public tasks contain issues that can distort results. Some correct solutions fail because of hidden requirements, contradictory instructions, overly strict tests, or incomplete grading criteria.

updates.author: OpenAI
updates.text: We audited SWE-Bench Pro, one of the most widely used AI coding benchmarks, and found it no longer reliably measures frontier coding capability. We find 30% of SWE-Bench Pro tasks to be broken, and are retracting our previous recommendation that the research community use it as a leading coding eval. openai.com/index/separating-…

updates.author: Jim Fan (范瑞杰)
updates.text: ENPIRE - ASPIRE, our 2nd work in the series for Physical AutoResearch. We are building the components for robot self-improvement, one /skill at a time.

updates.author: Jim Fan (范瑞杰)
updates.text: Project gallery and whitepaper: research.nvidia.com/labs/gea… ASPIRE is a great collaboration between NVIDIA GEAR lab, UMich, Berkeley, and CMU. Kudos to all the coauthors who pour their hearts into the project! Check out the deep dive thread from Guanzhi:

updates.author: Jim Fan (范瑞杰)
updates.text: Today, we give robots a /skills library that self-evolves and compounds indefinitely! Introducing ASPIRE: a robot solving its 100th task is no longer as clueless as solving its first. Coding agents observe multimodal sensory traces from simulation and real robots, launch an evolutionary search over control programs, and distill the best know-how into an ever-expanding library. ASPIRE is a new type of continual learning: "training" is skill refinement instead of gradient descent. "Trained model"

updates.author: Jim Fan (范瑞杰)
updates.text: I made Physical AutoResearch sound simple (conceptually), but it took a village to pull off and lots of design thinking into the robot /loopcraft. The hardest part is everything we need to setup *before* pressing Enter. Here's a behind-the-scene tour: 1. Safety harness Letting 8 robots run unattended overnight means safety has to be more than a hint in the system prompt. ENPIRE hardwires it in 2 layers: (1) hard kinematic limit that trips an immediate task failure and auto-resets as soon as a ro

updates.author: Jim Fan (范瑞杰)
updates.text: Project site: research.nvidia.com/labs/gea… Wenli has written an excellent technical thread, please check it out!

updates.author: Ilya Sutskever
updates.text: It’s extremely good that Anthropic has not backed down, and it’s siginficant that OpenAI has taken a similar stance. In the future, there will be much more challenging situations of this nature, and it will be critical for the relevant leaders to rise up to the occasion, for fierce competitors to put their differences aside. Good to see that happen today.

updates.author: Ilya Sutskever
updates.text: One point I made that didn’t come across: - Scaling the current thing will keep leading to improvements. In particular, it won’t stall. - But something important will continue to be missing.

updates.author: Ilya Sutskever
updates.text: Important work

updates.author: Ilya Sutskever
updates.text: truly the greatest day ever🎗️

updates.author: Google DeepMind
updates.text: The Predicting the Past Skill is a collaborative research partner. 🏛️ We teamed up with @TSommerschield on 3️⃣ case studies to prove it. By blending specialized AI with Gemini’s reasoning, we showed how historians can explore ancient data in entirely new ways. Explore the platform → goo.gle/4vR5ZtC

updates.author: Google DeepMind
updates.text: Using AI for history analysis has 3 core challenges: 🔹 Creating custom analysis and visuals for every inscription. 🔹 Cross-source mapping to find large-scale patterns. 🔹 Utilizing advanced AI tools with no coding skills. Our Antigravity skill shifts all these complex workflows into plain English.

updates.author: Google DeepMind
updates.text: 🏛️ We’re unveiling a new way to converse with the ancient world. By grounding Gemini directly in our expert models Aeneas and Ithaca, our Predicting the Past Skill in Google @antigravity lets historians study Greek and Latin texts using plain English. 🧵

updates.author: Google DeepMind
updates.text: Find out more → goo.gle/4oTOq9I

updates.author: Google DeepMind
updates.text: Pair these models together using the Interactions API. 🤝 Quickly generate an image with Nano Banana 2 Lite, then immediately animate it using Gemini Omni Flash. Plus, you can maintain session history to stack up to three sequential edits.

updates.author: Demis Hassabis
updates.text: Thanks John for an extraordinary partnership and wonderful collaboration over the past 9 years! What we achieved with AlphaFold changed the world, and showed the field what was possible with AI for science and medicine, lighting the way for how AI can benefit humanity.

updates.author: Demis Hassabis
updates.text: Awesome to see this innovation in text diffusion. DiffusionGemma is lightning fast, 4x faster than other Gemma 4 models! Congrats to @bodonoghue85 and the team who worked so hard on this - excited to see what people build with it!

updates.author: Demis Hassabis
updates.text: Celebrating the milestone of a massive 150+ million downloads of Gemma 4 with the release of the new Gemma 4 12B model! It's incredibly powerful for such a small model and it’s tiny enough to run locally on a laptop with just 16GB VRAM. Apache 2.0 license - happy building!

updates.author: Andrej Karpathy
updates.text: This is a new paradigm for interacting with Claude that is significantly more "inline" with all the other human activity org-wide. Once you do all of the under the hood engineering work to make this "just work" (e.g. across tools, integrations, compute environments, memory, security, etc.), Claude basically joins the team in a seamless way - you can talk to it as you would talk to a person and it can help with a very large variety of workloads. Imo this is the 3rd major redesign of LLM UIUX. The

updates.author: Andrej Karpathy
updates.text: In awe of SpaceX and its story - past, present and the future. You can think about it in 10+ different ways and continue re-blowing your mind in circles. Huge congrats to the team! 🚀

updates.author: Andrej Karpathy
updates.text: This is a super exciting release - Claude Fable 5 is the same underlying model as Mythos but with added safeguards. The benchmarks are great and it's SOTA on everything by a margin but I'll add that *qualitatively* also, this is a major-version-bump-deserving step change forward (imo of the same order as Claude 4.5 was in November), peaking especially for long problem-solving sessions on very difficult problems. You can give it a lot more ambitious tasks than what you're used to, the model "gets

updates.author: Andrej Karpathy
updates.text: Personal update: I've joined Anthropic. I think the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be especially formative. I am very excited to join the team here and get back to R D. I remain deeply passionate about education and plan to resume my work on it in time.

updates.author: Andrej Karpathy
updates.text: This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the generated file in your browser. I've also had some success asking the LLM to present its output as slideshows, etc. More generally, imo audio is the human-preferred input to AIs but vision (images/animations/video) is the preferred output from them. Around a ~third of our brains are a massively parallel processor dedicated to vision, it is the 10-lane superhighway of information
